turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Hey B, so uh, about the Horizon app project... we've poured like, what, 2.5 million into it already, right? I mean, we're seeing the user numbers tanking, but... shouldn't we just keep going? Sunk cost, you know?

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Yeah, Alex, I've seen the latest metrics. It's rough. But 2.5 million? That's exactly why we should reconsider. Throwing good money after bad feels like... I don't know, digging a deeper hole.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: But come on, think about it. We've already built the core engine, the UI is polished... if we pull out now, that's 2.5 million down the drain. Totally. What do we even tell the board? 'We quit because it got hard'?

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: I get that, it's frustrating. Remember last quarter when we pivoted on the ad model? That cost us extra, but... wait, no, that's not helping. Look, sunk cost fallacy, right? We've talked about this in training. Just because we've spent it doesn't mean we spend more.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Fallacy, shmallacy. Haha, okay, but seriously, uh... our competitors are still in this space. If we bail, someone else scoops it up. And we've got the team here, idle if we stop. That's another cost, layoffs or whatever.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: True, the team... yeah, that's a tangent, but fair. Reassigning them to something viable might be better. Have you run the numbers on opportunity cost? Like, what if we shifted that budget to the AI chatbot thing? Projections there are promising.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: AI chatbot? Eh, that's unproven. Horizon at least has... potential? I mean, we were at 50k users peak. With another 500k, we break even on the 2.5 mil. We just need better marketing, maybe.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: 50k peak was months ago, and it halved since. Hesitant to say this, but... projecting another 500k feels optimistic. Remember the beta feedback? Users hated the battery drain. Fixing that's another 300k easy.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Battery drain, yeah... we could optimize that. But again, we've spent so much on the backend already. Scrapping it means rewriting from scratch elsewhere. That's not free.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: I hear you on the sunk cost, Alex. It's emotional. Like, uh, that time I kept fixing my old car instead of buying new? Wasted months. Point is, let's model it out. If we invest another mil, what's the ROI look like realistically?

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Okay, fine, let's say... pessimistic case, we get to 100k users, revenue covers half. Still better than zero. And the IP? We own it. Sell it off if it flops.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: IP value's low if it's failing. Tangent: speaking of selling, I saw that startup acquired a similar dud for scraps last week. But yeah, maybe. Still, I think we cut losses. Propose we vote on it next meeting?

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Vote? Ugh, alright... but I'm noting the 2.5 mil we've already committed. Can't ignore that.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Noted, but future matters more. Let's crunch those numbers before then.

input.objective: Debating whether to continue funding the failing Horizon app project, with sunk cost arguments
